representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins by expanding a single input state into three candidate paths (K2, M9, R4). All three paths converge at a step that introduces a differentiating input (T6). A sorting and evaluation step (X1) then re-ranks the paths based on the new input. If one path is clearly dominant, the procedure closes through V3 and exits. If separation among paths remains weak, a re-evaluation node (Q8) reopens one or more of the candidate paths and the loop repeats. If continuation is determined to be unjustified, the procedure exits directly from X1 without selection. The loop has no stated upper bound.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: fan-out to parallel candidates -> inject differentiating input -> evaluate and rank -> conditional: select-and-close | reopen-and-loop | terminate-without-selection

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: Graph topology (fan-out, parallel paths, convergence, evaluate-and-select loop) is consistent with multiple known procedure families including hypothesis generation and testing, beam search, ensemble selection, and multi-hypothesis tracking. Node renumbering removes label-based recognition cues. No domain terminology is present in the packet. The combination of features is suggestive but not uniquely identifying.
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: low; no domain terms were introduced; structural observations are grounded solely in graph edges and node notes; the parallel-path-with-reeval topology is generic enough that no specific artifact was assumed

confidence.constraint_layer: medium-low; preconditions and validity conditions are largely inferred; threshold and stopping criteria values are absent
confidence.representation_layer: medium; AST is structurally faithful but parallel-vs-sequential ambiguity at A7 and the three-way branch semantics at X1 reduce precision

confidence.overall: medium; packet is internally consistent and graph is fully traversable, but key procedural parameters (thresholds, selection logic, path execution model) are absent

notes: The packet is well-formed and the graph is unambiguous at the edge level. The primary sources of extraction uncertainty are: (1) the execution model for the A7 fan-out (parallel vs. sequential vs. speculative); (2) the three-way branch semantics at X1, particularly the relationship between 'threshold not met' and 'continuation not justified'; (3) the selection logic at Q8 when returning to K2/M9/R4. These ambiguities are recorded explicitly rather than resolved arbitrarily. No domain terminology was introduced. Recognition was not achieved.
